### Structural Description of Twin Primes
A twin prime pair is an unordered pair of primes $\{p, p+2\}$. The only such pair including 2 or 3 is $(3,5)$. All other twin prime pairs take the form $\{6k-1, 6k+1\}$ for some integer $k\geq1$, since all primes greater than 3 are congruent to $\pm1\pmod{6}
